my_thoughts: The functional definition works: lunatic = false values + formatory thinking (technical definition), links: false values (imaginary qualities), formatory thinking (mechanical). The student proposes definition (seeking functional definition), which shows they understand: lunatic = wants things out of proportion (definition). They'll likely ask about archetypal definition next. Hypothesis: functional definitions trigger archetypal questions—testable by tracking whether functional definitions lead to archetypal inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student proposes definition ('Is one of the features of a lunatic that he wants certain things out of proportion to other things, in such a way that it will be bad for him as A whole?')—seeking functional definition via proposes definition and seeks confirmation. They understand: lunatic = wants things out of proportion (definition). The none mechanism provides: lunatic = false values + formatory thinking (technical definition), links: false values (imaginary qualities), formatory thinking (mechanical). Pattern: definition proposal → functional definition → [next: archetypal question]. Low confidence because definition proposals can lead to clarification requests or compliance.
visible_response:
'Lunatic' means a man who always runs after false values, who has no right
discrimination.
He is always formatory... lunatics are particularly devoted to formatory thinking: that is their chief affection in
one way or another.
